title: Promote L2 round-trip and L3 sync-ordering tests into verify.py's keystone GATE_TESTS
source: Claude Fable improvement pass 2026-07-07 - write/read divergence hit its 6th confirmed instance this week (T-270 L3 sync truncation); the keystone gate exists precisely for this class but only covers L3/L1
current_state: scripts/verify.py's GATE_TESTS (line ~47) - the keystone tests that ALWAYS run first and hard-fail, with a fail-fast if the file goes missing - contains test_conversation_golden.py, test_context_fidelity.py, and test_memory_roundtrip_contract.py (L3/L1 tier round-trip). Two sibling guards for the same #1 recurring bug class exist but run only in the ordinary suite: testing/test_l2_roundtrip_contract.py (L2 write->read content preservation, T-167) and testing/test_l3_sync_ordering.py (the T-270 guard: _sync_l3 orders newest-first and caps rows - the exact bug that silently dropped new memories in production). Ordinary tests can be skipped by COSTLY_TESTS edits or accidental exclusion; keystones cannot.
target_state: GATE_TESTS includes both files. The gate's missing-file fail-fast now also protects them (the safety net that catches memory divergence can never silently disappear from CI). A comment on the tuple names the class each entry guards. Acceptance: verify.py output shows 'gate: 5 run, 0 failed' and deleting either file makes verify FAIL before the main suite starts.
migration_plan: 1. Confirm both tests are fast and hermetic (they are - recording doubles / in-memory stores; re-verify no live API use so gate time stays low).
2. Add the two filenames to GATE_TESTS in scripts/verify.py with one-line comments naming the guarded class.
3. Run python scripts/verify.py - expect 'gate: 5 run, 0 failed' and overall PASS (never pipe, T-214).
risk_notes: scripts/ only - SAFE component, verified, trivially testable: a genuine sprint.py candidate (arguably the smallest in the queue - a good rehearsal ticket). Do not add more than these two: the gate must stay fast enough to run first on every verify; keystones are for emergent-property guards, not a second full suite.
